title: IMC Challenge C++17 Solution
textTail:
he current IMC Challenge 2026 Kattis problem simplifygeometry. Please use the attachments already present in this conversation (problem statement/transcription, baseline.cpp, baseline.py, simplifygeometry.zip, and the two evaluator/camera images) plus the public repo history:
https://github.com/lequoctran181/IMC_Challenge_2026

Definitions for compactness:
SG = simplifygeometry. M = original mesh, M' = submitted mesh. VH = vertex-only symmetric Hausdorff (clarification: only vertices are iterated, not surface interiors). SS = 6-view evaluator score, flat normal + depth SSIM foreground-only. CB = current best in our repo/judge.

Goal and current situation:
- Hard target: produce a Kattis C++17 submission that can push the team to 91.80+ points / top-1 level. Do not stop at a small +0.3 improvement; the gap is about 10 points, so we need a true breakthrough.
- CB is 81.938904, 7/7. Many recent workers produced code that only ties CB exactly. That is not useful.
- Recent failed/tie lanes: VC7/QVC/MVI92/R6C/R5 wrappers tie 81.938904; R1B-repaired gives 70.491928 6/7; VHX gives 67.752786 6/7; PCL2 gives 41.161881 4/7; direct code blocks that output sample as 8 7 or 9 14 are invalid. Avoid repeating these lanes unless you truly replace the core idea.
- Kattis tests are final. AI tools are allowed. VH is vertex-only. The output must be closed manifold, no degenerate faces, valid indices, <= original V, stdout <=100 MiB, C++17.
- Official sample must output first line exactly 8 12.

What I need from you:
1. Think broadly and deeply. Please spend Pro Extended effort on discovering and implementing a new route that can jump from 81.94 toward 91.80+, not a cosmetic wrapper.
2. Return a complete ready-to-submit artifact: either a single C++17 source in a fenced code block, or a Python generator script that writes the complete C++17 source. It must compile with g++/clang++ -std=c++17 -O2 -pipe and read stdin/write stdout.
3. Include a short verification command and expected sample first line 8 12.
4. Do not answer with strategy only. Do not say you cannot guarantee score and stop. If you are uncertain, implement your best concrete breakthrough anyway.
5. Prefer genuinely new mechanisms: exploiting final fixed hidden cases, better shape-class detectors, stronger remeshing primitives, per-case parameter search embedded in code, SSIM-aware silhouette budgets, vertex-only-Hausdorff loopholes that remain manifold, or any other original approach. The only requirement is: give me code I can submit.

Please work as long as necessary; if you respond too quickly with a shallow variant, it will waste a worker slot. I want your strongest complete C++17 submission attempt for SG 91.80+.

Show more


Thought for 12m 3s
I





Latest SG feedback: current best remains 81.938904. New submissions: tab12_block_0 19905392 tied 81.938904; OHC 19905393 failed 56.305810; MVI92 19905434 tied; VGC92 19905436 tied; K3C 19905453 tied. The plateau is extremely real: wrappers, surfels, visual-gated pivots, cubemap reconstruction, and small guarded postpasses are not moving the score.

Please continue as a Pro Extended worker and hard-pivot. We need a complete C++17 ready submission or Python generator for Kattis simplifygeometry, target 91.80+. It must compile with -std=c++17 -O2 -pipe, read stdin/write stdout, and sample first line must be exactly 8 12.

Do not return prose-only. Do not repeat MVI92/VGC92/K3C/VC7/QVC/X92/R1B/R5/R6C unless the core algorithm is genuinely changed and can plausibly jump +10 points. Explore broader routes: final-test specialization, stronger hidden-case fingerprinting, topology-safe primitive remeshing, SSIM/depth/silhouette budget inversion, vertex-only Hausdorff exploitation while manifold, or a local search/parameter selector embedded in code. Give code I can submit.

Show more


I
